caption: The image displays a series of lecture slides titled "CRYOGENIC ENGINEERING" and "Tutorial – 3," presented by Prof. M D Atrey of the Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Bombay. An NPTEL logo appears in the bottom left corner of each frame. The slides focus on "Liquid yield," featuring a T-S (temperature-entropy) diagram with colored lines indicating constant temperature and pressure processes. A mathematical formula for liquid yield, $y = \frac{h_1 - h_2}{h_1 - h_r}$, is shown in a blue box. As the sequence progresses, a data table appears at the bottom of the diagram. This table lists values for pressure $p$ (bar), temperature $T$ (K), enthalpy $h$ (j/g), and entropy $s$ (j/gK) across different states labeled "1", "2", and "f". The frames show the progression of the lecture as these technical details are introduced to explain the cryogenic process.
